title: Brepols - On Holy Ground: Liturgy, Architecture and Urbanism in the Cathedral and the Streets of Medieval Florence
highlights:
### Franklin Toker

- Illustrations:52 b/w
- Size:220 x 280 mm
- Pages: 324 p.  ...    On Holy Ground: Liturgy, Architecture, and Urbanism in the Cathedral and in the Streets of Medieval Florence asks just one question: had the Florence Duomo never been excavated, what could we have known of the legendary cathedral of S. Reparata below it? The answer comes through the transcription of two key texts: one, never published until now, was written for the cathedral clergy around 1190; the other was composed around 1230, and printed just once, in the  ...    pageantry of Christmas to the plaintive rites of Easter. The archaeological finds now make sense of the chapels,  ...    One chapter is devoted to the religious processions that ventured forth from S. Reparata
